title: Friday Sermon 23 June 1995
description:
With reference to the start of the Jalsas [Annual Conferences] of the Ahmadiyya Community in America and Indonesia I convey my greetings of Assalamo Alaikum to the attendees and on behalf of the worldwide Ahmadiyya Communityand I pray for the success of these Jalsas.  I draw the attention of the Community in both these nations to give special attention to bringing about extraordinary changes in their proselytising activities.

Those who become people of the Truth they are granted magnanimous victories over people and pure changes occur in them by connecting to the Truthful One as would be expected.  

When the truth appears falsehood automatically runs away and one example of this is that of the rising sun which banishes the dark patches of night.  But we would have to put in a lot of toil as far as proselytising is concerned.  To turn those nights which are known as spiritual darkness into daylight, is a task that requires a lot of effort.  One has to illuminate the nights by connecting to the light of Almighty God.  This is not a light which would come up out on its own.  That is because falsehood puts its forces to use so that it may be blocked and eradicated.  So the image of preaching that here one proclaims a message and there people would accept it, this is not mentioned in 'The Holy Quran'.

In the light of the Quranic verses and the history of Ahmadiyya the enemies of truth have utilised every effort in order to wipe out the truth but those who are connected to the Truth even though they are few triumph over the false majority and those who rise with an endevour to erase them are themselves erased.  In this way it is evident to the world that they are upon the truth and Allah Almighty verifies the Truth through His Words.  

There is another subject related to this that the Truth would only be vouchsafed when something is connected to it.  As soon as that bond with the truth is broken the testimony of Allah Almighty that 'Truth has come and falsehood has fled'* regarding the truth would cease to be fulfilled in our favour.  However when you will become a fruitful branch Allah Almighty would certainly stand in your support.  In order to become a successful preacher one requires wisdom and not cowardice.  Every Prophet was a granted gnosis.  So one cannot preach without wisdom whatsoever.  Allah Almighty places a condition of wisdom at the very beginning of preaching.  But there is no condition of fear mentioned anywhere.  In fact Almighty God eradicates fear by saying 'I am with you'.  So unless the signs of truth are born in you you cannot appear before the world as a fearless and careless summoner to Allah.  So if you connect with the Truth in this manner you will see what great transformations occur in your preaching.

*'The Holy Quran'.  Bani Israel [Children of Israel]: 82).
